1990-08-11-India-Mumbai-Humility-BG-18-55-st2-vr1

Meta Data v3

Event Name

Humility Bhagavad Gita 18.55

Event Date

11 Aug 1990

Event Location

Mumbai, India

Event Type

Temple - BG class

Transcriber

Pushpanjali dd

Completed

17 Dec 2020

Source Audio

https://radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-061-Humility (Mumbai)-BG_18_55-dt_90_08_11.mp3

Source Video

Verse

BG 18.55

Places

Yagna-stana, Orissa, Kurmakshetra

Pastimes

Story of yagnic brahmins and brahmin patnis, mercy to King Prataparudra and Vasudev, Maharaj Parikshit takes shelter

Persons

Krishna, gopas, yagnic brahmins, brahmin patnis, Lord Chaitanya, King Prataparudra, Vasudev, Sukadev Goswami, Maharaj Parikshit

Themes

Humility, bhakti only way to know Krsna, bhakti is love, power of association, death, inquire submissively, render service

Lecture Transcription

bhaktyā mām abhijānāti
yāvān yaś cāsmi tattvataḥ
tato māṁ tattvato jṣātvā
viśate tad-anantaram

[Bg 18.55]

[One can understand Me as I am, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, only by devotional service. And when one is in full consciousness of Me by such devotion, he can enter into the kingdom of God.]

Purport by his divine grace Srila A.C. Bhakti Vedanta Swami Prabhupada

The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Krsna, and His plenary portions cannot be understood by mental speculation nor by the non-devotees. If anyone wants to understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he has to take to pure devotional service under the guidance of a pure devotee. Otherwise, the truth of the Supreme Personality of Godhead will always be hidden. As already stated in Bhagavad-gītā (7.25), nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasya: He is not revealed to everyone. No one can understand God simply by erudite scholarship or mental speculation. Only one who is actually engaged in Krsna consciousness and devotional service can understand what Krsna is. University degrees are not helpful.

One who is fully conversant with the Krsna science becomes eligible to enter into the spiritual kingdom, the abode of Krsna. Becoming Brahman does not mean that one loses his identity. Devotional service is there, and as long as devotional service exists, there must be God, the devotee, and the process of devotional service. Such knowledge is never vanquished, even after liberation. Liberation involves getting free from the concept of material life; in spiritual life the same distinction is there, the same individuality is there, but in pure Krsna consciousness. One should not mistakenly think that the word viśate, “enters into Me,” supports the monist theory that one becomes homogeneous with the impersonal Brahman. No. Viśate means that one can enter into the abode of the Supreme Lord in one’s individuality to engage in his association and render service unto him. For instance, a green bird enters a green tree not to become one with the tree but to enjoy the fruits of the tree. Impersonalists generally give the example of a river flowing into the ocean and merging. This may be a source of happiness for the impersonalist, but the personalist keeps his personal individuality like an aquatic in the ocean. We find so many living entities within the ocean, if we go deep. Surface acquaintance with the ocean is not sufficient; one must have complete knowledge of the aquatics living in the ocean depths.

Because of his pure devotional service, a devotee can understand the transcendental qualities and the opulence of the Supreme Lord in truth. As it is stated in the Eleventh Chapter, only by devotional service can one understand. The same is confirmed here; one can understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead by devotional service and enter into His kingdom.

After attainment of the brahma-bhūta stage of freedom from material conceptions, devotional service begins by one’s hearing about the Lord. When one hears about the Supreme Lord, automatically the brahma-bhūta stage develops, and material contamination – greediness and lust for sense enjoyment – disappears. As lust and desires disappear from the heart of a devotee, he becomes more attached to the service of the Lord, and by such attachment he becomes free from material contamination. In that state of life, he can understand the Supreme Lord. This is the statement of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam also. After liberation the process of bhakti, or transcendental service, continues. The Vedānta-sūtra (4.1.12) confirms this: ā-prāyaṇāt tatrāpi hi dṛṣṭam. This means that after liberation the process of devotional service continues. In the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, real devotional liberation is defined as the reinstatement of the living entity in his own identity, his own constitutional position. The constitutional position is already explained: every living entity is a part-and-parcel fragmental portion of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, his constitutional position is to serve. After liberation, this service is never stopped. Actual liberation is getting free from misconceptions of life.

Translation

bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaś cāsmi tattvataḥ. In the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Sri Krsna explains

bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya

aham evaṁ-vidho ’rjuna

jṣātuṁ draṣṭuṁ ca tattvena

praveṣṭuṁ ca paran-tapa

[Bg 11.54]

[My dear Arjuna, only by undivided devotional service can I be understood as I am, standing before you, and can thus be seen directly. Only in this way can you enter into the mysteries of My understanding.]

Krsna can be understood only by love and devotion

It is only by undivided devotion that I reveal myself as I am. The supreme absolute truth bhagavan is beyond the perception of our human senses or mind. The supreme absolute truth Narayana is beyond the realm of relativity. Therefore, by any mechanical process of yoga he is not attainable. He is only perceived when by his own divine sweet will, he reveals himself to his devotee.

So here we are tonight for the last one hour or so, we have been chanting the Holy names of the Lord. In the age of Kalyuga, this process of Naam sankirtana is specially recommended. mandāḥ sumanda-matayo

manda-bhāgyā hy upadrutāḥ [Srimad-Bhagavatam 1.1.10]

[O learned one, in this iron Age of Kali men almost always have but short lives. They are quarrelsome, lazy, misguided, unlucky and, above all, always disturbed.]

Bhagavatam explains in the age of Kali men are very spiritually lazy, they have very short memories, very short lives, they are very much easily influenced by materialistic attachments and sinful activities. Therefore, it is said in Bhagavatam,

kaler doṣa-nidhe rājann

asti hy eko mahān guṇaḥ

kīrtanād eva kṛṣṇasya

mukta-saṅgaḥ paraṁ vrajet

[Srimad-Bhagavatam 12.3.51]

[My dear King, although Kali-yuga is an ocean of faults, there is still one good quality about this age: Simply by chanting the Hare Kṛṣṇa mahā-mantra, one can become free from material bondage and be promoted to the transcendental kingdom.]

Essence of bhakti is love and humility

Although this age of Kaliyuga is an ocean of faults, how we can see the ocean of faults all around us, there is one benediction that anyone simply by taking the name of Lord Hari, he can attain the supreme perfection of life. But how do we take the name of Lord Hari, that is what is essential to understand. Sometimes we have our mala and we have a fixed number of names that we chant every day. It may be 1 lakh or 2 lakhs or 3 lakh or quarter lakh or half lakh. Our Guru Maharaj is giving us a particular regiment of how many names we should chant per day but do you think that Krsna would be attracted by a number. We are tonight chanting, dancing, do you think he is attracted by the sound that is coming out of our voice? God is only attracted by one thing bhakti. Bhakti means love, devotion. Devotion is manifested or expressed through sacrifice and sacrifice is offered to the Lord through seva, through service. Unless we have the proper service attitude our chanting has no meaning to God. Just like you may have a gun, today so many people have guns and do you know what is blanks, blanks mean a certain type of gunpowder that does not have a bullet at its end. It just makes the noise; it sounds like the gun – (gunshot sounds-kha kha kha) but nothing comes out. Chanting the Holy name of the Lord without proper service attitude is like the sound of the blank gun. It sounds just like the name of God, but is it really the name of God? The name of God attracts the Lord only when it is chanted with a heart of humility. Therefore, Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has given us a particular verse which he considered most essential.

tṛṇād api sunīcena

taror api sahiṣṇunā

amāninā mānadena

kīrtanīyaḥ sadā hariḥ

[Cc. Ādi 17.31, Śikṣāṣṭaka 3]

[One who thinks himself lower than the grass, who is more tolerant than a tree, and who does not expect personal honor yet is always prepared to give all respect to others can very easily always chant the holy name of the Lord.]

To chant the name of the Lord properly one must be more humble than a blade of grass, one must be more tolerant than a tree, one must be ready to offer all respect to others and expect no respect for one’s self. Only when you have this consciousness can you chant the name of the Lord constantly. At this time the name of the Lord will not only be coming out of your mouth but it will be constantly vibrating from the core of your heart. This is what is required. Spirituality is not a mechanical process; it is not a process of ritual. It is a process of offering our love. When we come before the ‘archa vigraha’, the worshipable deity of the Lord, he so kindly appears in this most beautiful and lovable form just to reciprocate with his devotee. We may cook many many wonderful palatable dishes but if they are not prepared and offered with bhakti, with love, even if we spend crores of rupees, it means nothing to God. patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati [Bg 9.26]

[If one offers Me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water, I will accept it.]

Krsna says if you even offer me a little water, little fruits or a flower, if it is offered with love, with bhakti not only will he accept but he will find great pleasure.

Today the young generation all over the world is very much turning against religion because of the rise in science, the rise in technology. They feel what is the need for religion. They see their parents and they think that they are performing these rituals and when they ask what the rituals mean, generally they say, do not ask these questions. Your grandfather did it, your great grandfather did it, your great great grandfather did it, your great great great grandfather did it. Therefore, I must do it and you must do it. The scientific mind of the twentieth century will no longer accept this reasoning. There must be a philosophical explanation, a conviction and that philosophical conviction must bring about an expression of devotion, otherwise whatever mantras we are chanting, whatever rituals we are performing are like the sounds of blank bullets – (gunshot sounds-kha kha kha). They have no substance, they have no flavor, there is no rasa. Therefore, Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explained that the basic ingredient of all of our spiritual endeavors is that it must be performed with humility.

Story of yagnic brahmins

There is a story in the Srimad Bhagavatam tenth Canto, which very nicely illustrates this very important principle. Recently we were in Vrindavan and we went to the very place where this pastime occurred. It is called ‘yagya sthan’. Have you been? It’s just close to Akrura ghat. And this is the place where one day Lord Sri Krsna, when he was performing his pauganda leela, he was herding the cows with his friends, the sakhas and the gopas. Every day, they would have a very very joyful time. They would dance frolicking through the fields of Vraja, playing upon their flutes, blowing their buffalo horns. It is explained that Sri Krsna, the son of Nanda Maharaj was given the care of nine hundred thousand cows. Last night I asked a question, it is said, somebody asked me about the jhulan yatra, where Krsna and Radharani they swing together. Now Lord Sri Krsna and Srimati Radharani, they performed this jhulan yatra leela, when they are in their kaishore age, this means the age between eleven and fifteen. But Krsna left Vrindavan when he was between ten and eleven. Now it is sometimes said he left Vrindavan when he was sixteen, but actually if you tactically study his leela, you will find that when he was between the ages ten and eleven, he left with Akrura in order to fight with Kamsa. And yet the most intimate pastimes he had with Sri Radharani and the gopis was during his kaishore leela which is when he was a youth and fifteen years old. So how was this possible? It is explained by the great Acharyas that Nanda Maharaj had nine hundred thousand cows, of those nine hundred thousand cows the thousand best cows, he would milk and he would give all that milk to the hundred best cows. Then he would take the milk from the hundred best cows and give it to the ten best cows. Then he would take the milk from the ten best cows and give it to the five best cows and then he would take the milk from the five best cows and give it to the best of all cows and from that best of all cows he would take the milk and make yoghurt, make butter and that is what would be fed to Krsna every day. Now what would happen is because he was fed with the best milk and the best yoghurt and the best butter and the best ghee, every day, he became very stout and strong. So, when he was ten years old, he had a body like he was fifteen years old. So actually, he was kaishore when he was still in his pauganda leela.

So, one day when they were herding the beautiful nine hundred thousand cows, Lord Sri Krsna Gopal and Balaram were feeling very hungry, so they told their friends that there are some Brahmins, they are close by, they are performing a very very big sacrifice. They are very first-class Brahmins. They know the mantras so expertly; they know all the scriptures and they can speak with such eloquence. In fact, they are the gurus for all around. Everyone touches their feet and puts the dust on their head and just at this time when they are performing their sacrifice it is auspicious for them to give in charity. So, you should go and you should tell them that Krsna Balaram are very hungry, give us some Prasad. So, the cowherd boys they went to the place where the Brahmins were performing their sacrifice. Haan! Balaramji and Krsna, they are in the pasture grounds. They are very hungry. They are requesting some prasad, we know it is auspicious now, you will give it. The Brahmins, they were thinking that we are performing a very important sacrifice, we are in the middle of chanting very special mantras and these low class, low caste dirty little children who take care of cows, why are they bothering us. So, they completely ignored them. They said nothing, they did nothing and the children became very discouraged. So, with very very sad hearts they went back to Krsna. They said, “Shyamsunder they would not give us anything. We feel very bad”. Krsna explained to them you cannot be attached to the fruits of your action. When you are performing service you just perform your service for the sake of the service. Do not be attached to what is the result.

karmaṇy evādhikāras te

mā phaleṣu kadācana

mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr

mā te saṅgo ’stv akarmaṇi

[Bg 2.47]

[You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.]

Story of brahmin patnis

Are we ever really the cause of the results of our activities? Krsna told Arjuna, you fight for the sake of fighting. Understand that everything is the property of God, nothing is ours. Whatever we do have or whatever we do not have, that is not our concern. Our concern is that our life is an offering to God. So, then Krsna told the gopas that the wives of these Brahmins, they are not like their husbands. Their husbands are the greatest scholars. Their wives, I doubt if they even know how to write or read. Their husbands are famous throughout the land as being great lecturers, performing sacrifices and chanting mantras perfectly and expertly. The wives, all they know is how to clean the house and cook. Nobody has ever heard of them; nobody knows of them. But there is one difference, the husbands are proud. Therefore, whatever they do has no spiritual meaning. But the wives are humble and devoted. Go to them and ask for some food. So, the gopas went to the homes of the Brahmins’ wives. They told the brahmin’s wives that Krsna and Balaramaji are very hungry, they are requesting some food. Immediately the brahmins wives became very happy. Haan! such a benediction, such a good fortune that Krsna, Balarama are giving us the chance to offer them something! They ran throughout the house, choosing the best of all foods, putting it in the nicest baskets. Please my dear boys, let us kindly bring it personally to Lord Krsna. Meanwhile their husbands were saying haan, what is this nonsense! You have to help us with our sacrifice. You are required. Their fathers-in-law, their mothers-in-law, their husbands, they forbid them to go. But the brahmins wives they took the food in little baskets and they went into the forests of Vrindavan. And there when they came before the beautiful form of Shyamsunder, who was standing in his three-fold bending form, his head bedecked with a peacock feather, his eyes beautiful like the blooming lotus, his lips splendidly red like the bimba fruit. He was playing upon his flute and he was standing beside one of his friends. When they saw this beautiful darshan, immediately their hearts began to overflow with prema bhakti, pure love of God. At that time the supreme realization of the absolute truth was bestowed upon them and they offered their food to Krsna with humility and devotion. When their husbands found out about this, they became very ashamed. They were thinking here we are such learned scholars, here we are such famous gurus, but the whole purpose of everything we are doing is to satisfy God, satisfy Govinda and when the opportunity came, we were too proud. Therefore, to hell with our mantras, to hell with our rituals, to hell with all our vedic learning, to hell with our lives, we’ve spoiled everything. But our simple wives, they have perfected their lives. This is a fact.

King Prataparudra and Rath Yatra

There was once a king by the name Prataparudra. He was the king of Orissa. He had vast armies. He had tremendous, tremendous wealth, beautiful family. At that time Lord Sri Chaitanya was living in Jagannath Puri and the king wanted to have the darshan with Lord Chaitanya. Then Lord Chaitanya said I do not see kings, because kings are by nature very materialistic and proud. So, when Maharaj Prataparudra heard this, he was thinking then what is the use of my being a king, I should become a beggar, if being a beggar is what it takes to please the Lord. What else is this human life meant for? So, his associates knowing he was such a pious and such a religious man, they said no, do not become a beggar, just be patient. We think the Lord will show you his kindness. Soon after that the great festival of rathyatra, you know rathyatra, it was taking place and King Prataparudra, when he heard that Jagannath had boarded the cart, the rath, he took off his kingly robe, because he understood who am I to be dressed like a king in front of the supreme king. He is jagatpiteh, He is king of all the universe. Who am I? Actually, I was recently talking with one of the Acharyajis from Udupi, the Krsna temple. His name Palimar swami and they just offered the deity of Krsna a crown that cost one crore of rupees. And people were criticizing, why you spend one crore of rupees for Krsna. Why don’t you use the money for something else? He explained that the President of the United States, the Prime Minister of India, the Prime Minister of England or Russia, how much does their country spend on them, a year? Considering their travel arrangements, they have their own special private jets, considering all the high security guards that gather around him, all the technological information that protects them. They spend millions and millions and millions and millions and millions of dollars. It’s just a king of one little tiny track of land and one little tiny planet, in one little tiny universe. There are infinite innumerable universes and Krsna he’s the king of everything, everywhere. So, if you could spend so many crores on one little insect like king and one little insect like track of land, why can we not spend a crore of rupees on Jagatpateh, the king of everything. So, Maharaj Prataparudra he was in this consciousness that Jagannath, he is the king of the universe. Jagannath, I am only Orissa nath but he is Jagannath. Therefore, before the Lord he took off his royal dress and he took off his royal crown and he put on the robe of a street sweeper and he took a broom. And with a genuine and humble heart he began to sweep the road to show the whole kingdom that before God I am a bhangi, I am a street sweeper. I am only the most humble servant, the most insignificant servant. And when the Lord saw the humility of the king, it was then that he decided to bestow his complete mercy upon the king. It is the quality of our heart that is the offering that is accepted by God. It is not what we offer, but it is how it is offered.

Deliverance and humility of Vasudev, the leper

There is a beautiful story in the leela of Lord Chaitanya. There was a man, he was suffering from very, very, very severe leprosy. His leprosy was so bad, that his entire body was just dripping with horrible pus due to infections and there was millions and millions and millions of worms eating away at his flesh. Now when this man whose name was Vasudev, would see one worm fall to the ground, he would very carefully pick it up and put it back. He would think that this is not my body. This body belongs as much to the worms as it belongs to me. I’m living in this body; the worms are living in this body. This was his humility. So, one day Lord Chaitanya came to the place that he was staying. It was called Kurmakshetra, it is in south India. What happened is Vasudev came just after Lord Chaitanya had left, so Vasudev was thinking Oh my God! I have missed the opportunity of my life to have the association of such a great saint. So, Lord Chaitanya knowing this, he came back to that place and when he saw Vasudev, he embraced him. And when he embraced him all the leprosy disappeared and his body became beautiful, like the body of a devata. And then Vasudev, you are thinking would be very happy, would you not? After such leprosy, such pain. You were thinking, you would be going - Oh! so nice, now I can enjoy. He became very sad and he became very frightened and he approached the Lord and he said my Lord you know because you have shown me such a special degree of mercy, I will become famous everywhere as Vasudev Amritapad, as one who has received the special nectar of God. And the tendency is when you receive something great you become proud and I know my Lord if one drop of pride enters into my heart, then whatever I do for you, will have no pleasure for you. And my life is meant for your pleasure. When I was in a wretched sickly condition suffering as a leper, I had nothing to be proud of. The world condemned me, no one wanted to look at me, touch me or go near me. Therefore, whatever I did for you was with a humble heart, therefore I was quite happy. But now I have a beautiful strong, golden body and I’ve become very famous. Now my service will have no meaning to you. What is the use of my living? And the Lord explained to him that you always chant the name of God.

How to chant the name of God- feeling yourself more humble than the grass. People step on the grass. Dogs do whatever they do on the grass. Be more tolerant than a tree. What is the nature of the tree? A tree is so tolerant that in the summer the tree is standing in the blazing heat. Does it complain? No. It gives shade to you. In the winter the tree is freezing, standing. Does it complain? No. It gives wood for you to keep warm. And even if you chop the tree down to kill it, does it complain? No. It gives its body for you to build a house to protect your body. One should become more tolerant than a tree. Ready to offer all respect to others and expect none in return. This is the goal of life, to have this consciousness. Only then will we be truly satisfied. And how to do so? It is very simple.

‘sādhu-saṅga’, ‘sādhu-saṅga’---sarva-śāstre kaya

lava-mātra sādhu-saṅga sarva-siddhi haya

[Cc. Madhya 22.54]

[“The verdict of all revealed scriptures is that by even a moment’s association with a pure devotee, one can attain all success”.]

Power of association

You become what you associate with. Spiritual advancement and material degradation are exclusively dependent on what and who you associate with. If you associate with those persons who are sincerely aspiring for these qualities, while those persons who have by the grace of God, attained these qualities, then gradually you will also naturally acquire those qualities. But if you associate with those persons who are very much attached to the temporary flickering pleasures of this world, then your intelligence will be lost and you will become more and more and more attached to the flickering pleasures of this world.

This body must grow old, it must get diseased and it must die.

vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya

navāni gṛhṇāti naro ’parāṇi

tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāny

anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī

[Bg 2.22]

[As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.]

This body is only a set of clothes for the atma, the soul and yet we live for the body. We neglect the needs of the soul. We may call ourselves very pious, very religious, but how much are we actually living, moment after moment, for the soul and how much for the body? Recently, one person that we are acquainted with had left his body. He died. The soul passed on to its next destination. So, the family members were crying like anything. One sadhu came to visit. They asked the sadhu, please give us some enlightening words in this time of great, great lamentation in our life. Give us some comfort. He told them, how much you are lamenting over this dead body, the soul never dies. It is only the body that is dead. The inevitable has taken place, but let me tell you this, to forget Krsna, to forget the Lord, for one moment is more tragic than eight hundred deaths and you are lamenting so much over one death but moment after moment you forget Krsna and you do not even care. It is a fact.

Duty of man about to die

What is the quality of our life? A materialistic person would just try to enjoy as much as he possibly could, those last seven days. Right. He would just go on a big, big vacation like anything. He would try to squeeze as much sense enjoyment as he possibly could out of life. In fact, there was a television show in America about this. It was a very famous show when I was a young child. It was called “run for your life”. And what happened is, it was about this very wealthy, wealthy, wealthy lawyer. And he got cancer and the doctor told him that you have one year to live. So, he was thinking what will I do with the next year. I will enjoy such incredible amounts of pleasure during this last year of my life. So, every show, it was once a week for one hour, it showed how he would enjoy his last days. One week he would be in Switzerland climbing mountains with a beautiful young lady, the next week he would be in the Caribbean’s, skin diving in the ocean with a beautiful young lady, a different one of course every week. The next week he was in the Orient, aah, walking through the roads with a beautiful young lady. The next week he was jumping with parachutes from the sky off airplanes, with a beautiful young lady. In this way he was just enjoying like anything and people were thinking “my God this man is so great. If only I could spend my last year like this”. It was such a popular show because everybody in the American society was thinking that the goal of life is vishaya- sense enjoyment. And this man really knows how to live it up. This man, I want to become like him. Therefore, it became the number one show in America. It became such a very popular show that at the end of the year the sponsors were making so much of money that they had to extend his life one more year just so the show could continue.

But Maharaj Parikshit was not like this. He had seven days and what did he do with those seven days. He took off his royal crown, he took off his royal dress, he left his affectionate family members, he left his palatial palace, he went to the bank of the Ganges to seek out the association of saintly persons and he vowed I will not eat, I will not sleep, I will simply hear the words of the sadhus for the next seven days and I will keep my mind fixed on the sound vibration of Krsna. He approached Srila Sukadev Goswami. Such a contrast, here is the king of the planet earth, such dignity, such aristocracy, such sophistication he had and Sukadev Goswami was sixteen years old roaming through the jungles without any clothes. But yet the King put his head at the feet of Sukadev Goswami, because he understood that Sukadev Goswami has the ultimate wealth. He has the treasure of bhakti, treasure of love of God within his heart. And unless we attain that before we die our human life is wasted. Maharaj Parikshit understood that association is everything within one’s life. He asked Sukadev Goswami three questions. What is the supreme occupation for all humanity? What is the essence of all religions? And most important, what is the duty of a man about to die? The fact is every one of us, a man or a woman, is about to die. Death could come today, death could come tomorrow, we do not know. There is no guarantee. Maharaj Parikshit was guaranteed seven days. Can any of you tell me that you are guaranteed seven days? You do not even have a guarantee of seven minutes and yet how much we have the tendency to waste our time in futile pursuits. Sukadev Goswami explained to Maharaj Parikshit that to always hear about, glorify and remember the supreme Lord Sri Hari, that is the perfection of life. That is the duty of a man about to die, that is the supreme occupation for all humanity and that is the essence of all religions.

Krsna says in the Gita,

yaṁ yaṁ vāpi smaran bhāvaṁ

tyajaty ante kalevaram

taṁ tam evaiti kaunteya

sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ

[Bg 8.6]

[Whatever state of being one remembers when he quits his body, O son of Kuntī, that state he will attain without fail.]

Death, the final exam

Whatever you remember at time of death, you will attain without fail. One who remembers Me at the time of death will attain Me. Death is the final exam of life. Do not think that you will pass the exam unless you prepare throughout your life. Some people think well, at the time of death, then I will think of Krsna, but now let me enjoy. That is like a student going to college and saying Ah! why should I study, I will enjoy and when I take the exam then I will pass. Is that possible? To the degree you put your time and energy into study preparing for that exam, to that degree you will get good grade. So, our life is meant to prepare for the final exam which could come tomorrow. And how do we prepare? How does a student prepare for his exam in college? It’s very practical. Spiritual life is most practical. You prepare for the exam, by going to the class, by hearing from the professor with an open heart and you do what he says. Similarly, we prepare for the final exam of life by associating with saintly persons, by hearing from them and by following their instructions. Then what is their instruction? To always remember Krsna and never forget Krsna. And how do we always remember Krsna and never forget Krsna, by worshipping the deity, by putting the Lord in the center of our house. Just like this household, Mr. Dattani, he is setting a very wonderful example for everyone. It is not that one has to become a sanyasi.

gṛhe thāko, vane thāko, sadā ‘hari’ bole’ ḍāko,

sukhe duḥkhe bhulo nā’ko, vadane hari-nām koro

[Gay Gora Madhura Sware, 2]

[Whether you are a householder or a sannyasi, constantly chant "Hari! Hari!" Do not forget this chanting, whether you are in a happy condition or a distressful one. Just fill your lips with the harinama.]

Whether you are a grihasta or sanyasi, if you put God in the center of your life, you’ll be free from all the happiness and distress of this world and attain the divine position. So here we see that Krsna is in the center. This is not our house; this is Gopal’s house. We cook for Gopal, we live for Gopal, we work, we perform our occupation in the service of Gopal. This is human life.

The animals work because they want to eat, they want to sleep, they want to mate, they want to defend, right? The animals work for their own selfish pleasure. The difference between human life and animal life is, when a human being, he may have an occupation, when he works in the loving service of God, otherwise we are simply being big animals. So, when we worship the deity, when we offer everything we have to the deity, through this process we always remember Krsna, we never forget Krsna. When we associate with sadhus, when we come together to hear the glories of the Lord, to hear Bhagavatam, Bhagavad Gita, to hear it explained with an open heart, we always remember Krsna, we never forget Krsna. God is non-different than his name. When we chant the name of the Lord, whether we are chanting Sri Krsna sharanam mamam or whether we are chanting Hare Krsna Hare Krsna, Krsna, Krsna, Hare, Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama, Rama, Hare, Hare, by chanting the name of the Lord and surrendering our mind and our senses to the name of God, we are always remembering God. In this way 24 hours a day we can be conscious of Krsna. This is how we prepare for the final exam. And then when the exam comes, naturally, we have been taking shelter of God throughout our life, we have been offering everything throughout our life, at that time death is not a tragic condition. Death is simply the opening of the doors to the spiritual world Vrindavan. It is a blessed event. But for one who is not remembering Krsna, death is the most horrifying, terrible and painful experience. It is the time when everything near and dear to us is forcibly stripped away and we do not know where we are going. That is our choice. So therefore, Maharaj Parikshit has set the most perfect example, despite being the king of the whole planet earth, he humbled himself at the feet of God. He humbled himself at the feet of the Lords devotees. So, it does not matter who we are, whether we are educated or uneducated, whether we are man or woman, whether we are rich or poor, if we simply develop, if we simply strived to develop the attitude of humble service to the Lord and the Lord’s devotees, then there will be no impediment in our spiritual progress.

The Brahmins wives had only one qualification, the Brahmins had so many qualifications. They were like so many zeroes. All of your material qualifications are like so many zeroes. People in this society are very anxious to collect as many zeroes as they can. If you have a million zeroes, Ph.D. zero, M.A. zero, twenty crore rupees zeroes, fame and distinction, so many zeroes. If you add up a thousand zeroes times a million zeroes, what is the end result? Zero. At the time of death all you have is zero, nothing. But what if you put one in front of those zeroes, then whatever follows only increases the value. That one is Krsna and Krsna is only understood through humility and devotion. There’s no other way. That is the word of God. There’s no other way. So, whoever we are, whatever we are, let us understand what we should really strive for in our life. We should strive to associate with those persons and associate with those things that can awaken humble devotion in our heart.

Q&A Transcription

Are there any questions?

Q. We have heard many times, our minds still keep making plans for the future.

That means you haven’t ever really heard it. Hearing means acting. We have to hear it again and again and again and again till we actually hear it. Sometimes we hear the philosophy of Bhagavad Gita and we think we know it, but then by God’s grace we are put in a tragic situation and then we realize that we don’t know it. Then we begin to understand what it really meant.

tad viddhi praṇipātena

paripraśnena sevayā

upadekṣyanti te jṣānaṁ

jṣāninas tattva-darśinaḥ

[Bg 4.34]

[Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.]

One must approach Guru and inquire submissively and render service. You see we hear; we have to hear submissively. If you think you know you cannot hear. That is the greatest tragedy, when we think I am very intelligent, I know. So, I already heard this lecture so many times why should I listen again. That means you know nothing, except your own ego. I remember our Guru Maharaj, Srila Prabhupada, he would have his own disciples speak, he was a paramhansa. His own disciples, they were only devotees for few weeks, then they would just speak whatever little they knew. Bhagavad Gita says that you are not this body, you are like this, and Guru Maharaj would be attentively listening with great relish. If you think you know you cannot hear. Therefore, the beginning of spiritual life is to accept I do not know and open your heart to hear. But the realization comes through service, through seva. Hearing only gives you a theoretical understanding, but the realization comes through applying what you hear through seva, through service. It is attitude that is awakened when we hear. But with that service attitude we have to engage in devotional service. We have to act accordingly, then the realization comes. It’s like, you are a doctor, if you tell the patient exactly what medicine to take and he knows theoretically exactly what to take, will it do him any good? He has to take it. So similarly, we come to the pravachan, we hear about the goal of life, we hear how we should be associating with saintly persons, we hear how we should drop our false ego and become humble servant of God, we hear the necessity of chanting the name of God with feeling and devotion. We hear how to worship the deity with faith and devotion. We hear how to avoid worldly people. That is the prescription. But are you willing to take the prescription? Are you willing to practice what you hear? Until you do, you will gain no realization. You can hear it year after year, after year, after year, after year, after year, after year, but if you don’t practice what you hear, you don’t take it, there’ll be no realization. So, if there’s a lack of realization, that means there’s a lack of practice. You must apply these principles to your life.

Are there any other questions?

Q2. Can anyone take advantage of our humility?

No one can take advantage of humility. Real humility, you must understand. Arjuna fought the battle of Kurukshetra with complete humility. Did anyone take advantage of him? Humility doesn’t mean just letting people step all over you. Humility means understanding the principle that Krsna is the doer, I am only an instrument in His hands. Therefore, I deserve no credit for anything I do. All credit belongs to God. That if someone attacks me, if someone blasphemes me, I accept it, I welcome it, because it helps me to become more humble. But that humility means that you perform your duty without thinking that you are the enjoyer. You are not the enjoyer of the results. It is all meant for God. Arjuna fought in the state of mind that he was thinking himself more humble than a blade of grass. And yet he was killing his opponents. Why? Because that was his service to the Lord. Why should you care if someone takes advantage of you? Who are you? The question is you cannot allow someone to interfere with your service to God. We don’t care if people take advantage of us. Let them take advantage of us. But they cannot take advantage of God. We cannot permit that. Right? If this is God’s property, you cannot allow someone to take advantage of God’s property. But ourselves, we are not interested in protecting ourselves, we are interested in protecting what belongs to Krsna. If this is Krsna’s body, we are not protecting this because it is mine, we are protecting it because it is the property of God. My humility is to defend it on behalf of God. So, don’t care if someone criticizes you or takes advantage of you. Welcome it. There’s no harm, it can only help you. But your service to the Lord, you must defend with your life. That is also an expression of your humility.

Q3. Maharaj Parikshit during the last days of his life gave up everything and surrendered at feet of Sukadev Goswami. How should we develop our life in such a way?

By associating with persons who have that attitude of sacrifice, by hearing from them and rendering service to them.

The formula is simple, we have to take the formula.

Q4. We have so many ….[inaudible question 1:06:08.6], very difficult to sacrifice everything.

paripraśnena sevayā [Bg 4.34].

[Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.]

We must hear submissively from humble devotees of the Lord and we must render service to them. Through that simple process all good qualities awaken from within our hearts.

Any other questions?

So, I think we will end here. Thank you all very much.

Hare Krsna.
